publication.changeSummary: Misconception MCQ, canonical fill-gap, and runnable core task for Comprehensions; every item remains blocked from formal exams pending independent human review.

items.title: Comprehensions: reject the misconception
items.prompt: Which statement is source-aligned for Python: Beginner to Intermediate and corrects this misconception? A comprehension is always faster and more memory-efficient than a loop, regardless of result size or complexity.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted draft item; independent human source, technical, pedagogy, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Compare each option with both the language-specific boundary case and the cited operational model.

feedback.correct: Correct. Use a comprehension for a bounded readable transformation; choose an explicit loop for complex effects and a generator expression when lazy consumption is required.
feedback.incorrect: That choice preserves the misconception. Use a comprehension for a bounded readable transformation; choose an explicit loop for complex effects and a generator expression when lazy consumption is required.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned correction and rejects the documented misconception.

privateAuthorNotes: A human reviewer must verify source fidelity and ensure the distractor remains plausible without teaching it as fact.

options.text: Use a comprehension for a bounded readable transformation; choose an explicit loop for complex effects and a generator expression when lazy consumption is required.
A comprehension is always faster and more memory-efficient than a loop, regardless of result size or complexity.

answer.explanation: Use a comprehension for a bounded readable transformation; choose an explicit loop for complex effects and a generator expression when lazy consumption is required.

items.title: Comprehensions: canonical checkpoint

hints: Retrieve the short checkpoint phrase from the operational correction, then read the complete sentence for semantic fit.

feedback.correct: Correct. The checkpoint is 'bounded readable transformation'. Use a comprehension for a bounded readable transformation; choose an explicit loop for complex effects and a generator expression when lazy consumption is required.
feedback.incorrect: The phrase must preserve the exact language rule. Use a comprehension for a bounded readable transformation; choose an explicit loop for complex effects and a generator expression when lazy consumption is required.

criteria.description: Supplies the exact canonical checkpoint phrase that completes the source-aligned correction.

privateAuthorNotes: Human reviewer must verify the accepted phrase and whether additional spelling variants are technically equivalent.

items.title: Comprehensions: runnable core task
items.prompt: Read integers and use one readable comprehension to print the squares of only the even values.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted draft item; independent human source, technical, pedagogy, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Start from the stated input/output contract, implement the smallest language-specific operation, and run the visible boundary before changing structure.

feedback.correct: The visible and hidden Python: Beginner to Intermediate runner checks match the declared contract.
feedback.incorrect: At least one deterministic runner check differs; inspect input parsing, boundary behavior, and exact output before retrying.

criteria.description: Compiles or parses and passes every visible and hidden deterministic runner check for the declared contract.

privateAuthorNotes: Reference solution and both tests are AI-assisted draft material. Execute in the pinned runner and obtain independent human review before any exam use.

answer.explanation: The list comprehension is a bounded transformation with one expression and one readable filter.
